sources.name: Internal Usage Metrics (IUM)
sources.plainEnglish: Monthly tenant-level product usage and outcome metrics. This is the cleanest source for adoption and headline KPI trends when an IUM already exists.
sources.freshness: Live monthly feed. Current recruiting dashboards generally filter to SANDBOX.
usefulDataPoints: Add Documents configured / used / created (1757-1760 in current verified mapping)
pmNotes: Best when you need adoption or outcome trends by month, tenant, region proxy, or infrastructure.
Always cast `value` to numeric and note whether the dashboard uses SANDBOX or PROD.
Metric meanings can drift by ID over time, so verify the metric name before reusing an old query.

sources.name: Business Process Event Stats
sources.plainEnglish: One row per business process event with pre-computed duration and status. This is the primary source for sub-BP cycle time, completion, cancellation, and quality metrics.
sources.freshness: Live through today. Use PROD for real customer behaviour unless there is a clear reason not to.
usefulDataPoints: Completed average and median cycle time
Sent back / corrected / reassigned quality indicators
usedInDashboards: Job App Sub-BPs / BP Durations dashboard
pmNotes: Use this before `bp_event_record_stats` if the question is about whole-process performance.
Filter by status when analysing duration, or cancelled and in-progress work will distort the result.

sources.name: Business Process Step Records
sources.plainEnglish: Very large step-level event data across Workday business processes. Use it when you need task names, task durations, or step-level volumes that do not exist in the event-level table.
sources.freshness: Live through today, but expensive to query. Narrow the date range and task filters.
usefulDataPoints: Unique tenant coverage for a task
usedInDashboards: Offer step discovery for future dashboard additions
pmNotes: Confirmed Offer step names include `Generate Document`, `Review Documents`, `Review Writer Generated Document`, `Add Documents`, and `Manage Attachments`.
We have not yet validated a clean `Initiate Offer` task in this table, so do not chart it yet.
Because the table is huge, exploratory regex searches can take minutes and should be followed by narrower exact-name queries.

sources.name: OMS Request Logs
sources.plainEnglish: Detailed request logs for OMS tasks, including display names, durations, and request payload snippets. Useful for understanding what the application actually called when a user clicked through a workflow.
sources.freshness: Live log stream. Best for targeted discovery rather than broad trend dashboards.
usefulDataPoints: Potential attachment or document payload fields
usedInDashboards: No production dashboard yet - currently used for Offer step and attachment discovery
pmNotes: Good for proving task semantics before you put a metric on a dashboard.
Potential source for candidate attachment analysis via `processed_element_json` and `oms_request_json`, but we have not validated a stable attachment-type field yet.

sources.name: Document Repository Events
sources.plainEnglish: Repository-level document events with a document kind, reference id, and latency. Helpful when the question is about document storage or repository activity rather than the business process itself.
usefulDataPoints: Reference ids for possible correlation work
usedInDashboards: No production dashboard yet - reviewed for Offer attachment research
pmNotes: Current top `document_kind` values are operational document-system categories rather than clean candidate attachment types.
Treat this as a supporting source until we can prove a reliable join back to Offer tasks or attachment categories.

sources.name: Interview Initiation and Decision Events
sources.plainEnglish: Live interview-related event data for job applications and interview rounds. This is the main source for interview volume and time-in-process analysis.
pmNotes: Prefer this over older snapshot tables for active interview analysis.

sources.name: Interview Scheduling Events
sources.plainEnglish: Live scheduling task events for interview flows, including Schedule Interview and Schedule Interview Team.
usefulDataPoints: Segmentation by company size, region, and industry
pmNotes: Useful when the PM question is specifically about scheduling friction rather than the whole Interview BP.

sources.name: Interviewer Engagement
sources.plainEnglish: Rater and interviewer activity data, including ratings submitted and non-rating tasks.
pmNotes: Best for diagnosing interviewer participation rather than recruiter workflow speed.

sources.name: MISST Interaction Events
sources.plainEnglish: Page interaction events for Manage Interview Scheduling Settings. Useful for measuring how much effort recruiters spend inside MISST.
usefulDataPoints: Average total MISST time per requisition
pmNotes: Useful for productivity questions and aligns closely with the equivalent Superset view.

sources.name: Interview Dashboard Tenant Filters
sources.plainEnglish: Tenant-level segmentation and feature-adoption lookup table used to enrich metrics with company size, region, industry, and feature flags.
pmNotes: Use this as an enrichment join, not as a standalone trend source.

sources.name: Interview Session Enrichment Stats
sources.plainEnglish: Pre-aggregated interview session counts, including whether sessions used competencies or questionnaires.
pmNotes: Helpful when the PM wants a clean count metric without re-deriving enrichment joins.
